item.title: Investigating the intrinsic anomalous Hall effect in the topological semimetal ${\mathrm{MnPt}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jing Meng, Hongru Wang, Kun Zheng, Yuhao Wang, Zheng Li, Bocheng Yu, Haoyu Lin, Keqi Xia, Jingzhong Luo, Zengyao Wang, Xiaoyan Zhu, Baiqing Lv, Yaobo Huang, Jie Ma, Yang Xu, Shijing Gong, Tian Shang, and Qingfeng Zhan

The cubic ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{3}\mathrm{Au}$-type $X{\mathrm{Pt}}_{3}$ family ($X=\mathrm{V}$, Cr, and Mn) is a topological semimetal characterized by anticrossing gapped nodal lines near the Fermi level, which give rise to significant Berry curvatures and thus the to the anomalous Hall effect (AHE). Am…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134439] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jing Meng, Hongru Wang, Kun Zheng, Yuhao Wang, Zheng Li, Bocheng Yu, Haoyu Lin, Keqi Xia, Jingzhong Luo, Zengyao Wang, Xiaoyan Zhu, Baiqing Lv, Yaobo Huang, Jie Ma, Yang Xu, Shijing Gong, Tian Shang, and Qingfeng Zhan

The cubic Cu3Au-type XPt3 family (X=V, Cr, and Mn) is a topological semimetal characterized by anticrossing gapped nodal lines near the Fermi level, which give rise to significant Berry curvatures and thus the to the anomalous Hall effect (AHE). Among the three members, CrPt3 has been experimentally…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134439] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Investigating the intrinsic anomalous Hall effect in the topological semimetal ${\mathrm{MnPt}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Jing Meng, Hongru Wang, Kun Zheng, Yuhao Wang, Zheng Li, Bocheng Yu, Haoyu Lin, Keqi Xia, Jingzhong Luo, Zengyao Wang, Xiaoyan Zhu, Baiqing Lv, Yaobo Huang, Jie Ma, Yang Xu, Shijing Gong, Tian Shang, and Qingfeng Zhan

item.title: Simultaneous x-ray diffraction measurements of nine pressure calibrants to 140 GPa
item.description:
Author(s): Guoyin Shen and Jesse S. Smith

Accurate pressure calibration is fundamental to quantitative high-pressure science, yet inconsistencies among widely used secondary pressure scales persist. We report simultaneous synchrotron x-ray diffraction measurements on nine common pressure calibrants (Pt, Cu, Au, Mo, Ta, W, Fe, MgO, and NaCl)…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144113] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guoyin Shen and Jesse S. Smith

Accurate pressure calibration is fundamental to quantitative high-pressure science, yet inconsistencies among widely used secondary pressure scales persist. We report simultaneous synchrotron x-ray diffraction measurements on nine common pressure calibrants (Pt, Cu, Au, Mo, Ta, W, Fe, MgO, and NaCl)…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144113] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Simultaneous x-ray diffraction measurements of nine pressure calibrants to 140 GPa
item.dc:creator: Guoyin Shen and Jesse S. Smith

item.title: Signature of spin flop in magnetotransport: Case study of the intermediate-anisotropy antiferromagnet TbBi
item.description:
Author(s): Fang Tang, Yong Fang, Binchen Qu, Xunqing Yin, Shun Wang, Weiyao Zhao, Zhida Han, Sheng Ju, Renkui Zheng, and Tianyi Cai

Rare-earth monopnictides offer a unique testbed for exploring the entanglement of localized $4f$ moments with itinerant electrons. In this family, TbBi occupies a distinctive yet poorly understood position, characterized by intermediate magnetic anisotropy and a borderline topological electronic str…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144431] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fang Tang, Yong Fang, Binchen Qu, Xunqing Yin, Shun Wang, Weiyao Zhao, Zhida Han, Sheng Ju, Renkui Zheng, and Tianyi Cai

Rare-earth monopnictides offer a unique testbed for exploring the entanglement of localized 4f moments with itinerant electrons. In this family, TbBi occupies a distinctive yet poorly understood position, characterized by intermediate magnetic anisotropy and a borderline topological electronic struc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144431] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026

item.title: Ferrichiral skyrmions with sublattice-resolved chirality in an extended Kitaev model on the triangular lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Bogeng Wen, Jiefu Cen, and Hae-Young Kee

We study an extended Kitaev model on the triangular lattice in a limit where the symmetric off-diagonal bond-dependent and Heisenberg interactions together map onto an XXZ model, in addition to the Kitaev interaction. Within the previously identified ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ vortex regime, we uncover a fe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144432] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bogeng Wen, Jiefu Cen, and Hae-Young Kee

We study an extended Kitaev model on the triangular lattice in a limit where the symmetric off-diagonal bond-dependent and Heisenberg interactions together map onto an XXZ model, in addition to the Kitaev interaction. Within the previously identified Z2 vortex regime, we uncover a ferrichiral skyrmi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144432] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Ferrichiral skyrmions with sublattice-resolved chirality in an extended Kitaev model on the triangular lattice
item.dc:creator: Bogeng Wen, Jiefu Cen, and Hae-Young Kee

item.title: Kitaev meets Affleck-Kennedy-Lieb-Tasaki: Competing quantum disorder in spin-3/2 honeycomb systems
item.description:
Author(s): Sogen Ikegami, Kiyu Fukui, Rico Pohle, and Yukitoshi Motome

We investigate an $S=3/2$ quantum spin model on a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice that continuously interpolates between two paradigmatic quantum disordered states with distinct entanglement structures: the Kitaev quantum spin liquid and the Affleck-Kennedy-Lieb-Tasaki (AKLT) valence bond solid. C…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144433] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sogen Ikegami, Kiyu Fukui, Rico Pohle, and Yukitoshi Motome

We investigate an S=3/2 quantum spin model on a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice that continuously interpolates between two paradigmatic quantum disordered states with distinct entanglement structures: the Kitaev quantum spin liquid and the Affleck-Kennedy-Lieb-Tasaki (AKLT) valence bond solid. Com…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144433] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Kitaev meets Affleck-Kennedy-Lieb-Tasaki: Competing quantum disorder in spin-3/2 honeycomb systems
item.dc:creator: Sogen Ikegami, Kiyu Fukui, Rico Pohle, and Yukitoshi Motome

item.title: RKKY signatures as a probe for intrinsic magnetism and differentiation between axion insulator and quantum anomalous Hall phases in ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ films
item.description:
Author(s): Ya-Xi Li, Zi-Jian Chen, Rui-Qiang Wang, Ming-Xun Deng, Mou Yang, and Hou-Jian Duan

We present a systematic study of the Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida (RKKY) interaction in ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ films under both dark and illuminated conditions. In the dark, the intrinsic magnetism of ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ is shown to yield a stronger anisotropic RK…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155148] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ya-Xi Li, Zi-Jian Chen, Rui-Qiang Wang, Ming-Xun Deng, Mou Yang, and Hou-Jian Duan

We present a systematic study of the Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida (RKKY) interaction in MnBi2Te4 films under both dark and illuminated conditions. In the dark, the intrinsic magnetism of MnBi2Te4 is shown to yield a stronger anisotropic RKKY spin model compared to nonmagnetic topological insulators…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155148] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: RKKY signatures as a probe for intrinsic magnetism and differentiation between axion insulator and quantum anomalous Hall phases in ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ films
item.dc:creator: Ya-Xi Li, Zi-Jian Chen, Rui-Qiang Wang, Ming-Xun Deng, Mou Yang, and Hou-Jian Duan

item.title: Understanding Majorana braiding in superconductors with a fixed total number of particles
item.description:
Author(s): Ivar Martin and Kartiek Agarwal

Mean-field one-dimensional topological superconductors host edge Majorana zero modes (MZMs) that encode a topologically protected ground-state degeneracy and enable robust braiding operations within this subspace. Mean-field states lack definite particle number and thus cannot represent isolated sys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155149] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ivar Martin and Kartiek Agarwal

Mean-field one-dimensional topological superconductors host edge Majorana zero modes (MZMs) that encode a topologically protected ground-state degeneracy and enable robust braiding operations within this subspace. Mean-field states lack definite particle number and thus cannot represent isolated sys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155149] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Understanding Majorana braiding in superconductors with a fixed total number of particles

item.title: Visualizing the formation of flat bands in moiré superlattices of transition metal dichalcogenides
item.description:
Author(s): Shengguo Yang, Jiaxin Chen, Chao-Fei Liu, Ziming Zhu, and Mingxing Chen

Flat bands in moiré lattices of transition-metal dichalcogenides have emerged as a powerful platform for exploring strongly correlated electron physics. However, the moiré flat bands in these systems caused by band folding can originate from different valleys, which complicates the physics of the fl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155441] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shengguo Yang, Jiaxin Chen, Chao-Fei Liu, Ziming Zhu, and Mingxing Chen

Flat bands in moiré lattices of transition-metal dichalcogenides have emerged as a powerful platform for exploring strongly correlated electron physics. However, the moiré flat bands in these systems caused by band folding can originate from different valleys, which complicates the physics of the fl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155441] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026

item.title: Altermagnetism in an interacting model of kagome materials
item.description:
Author(s): Alejandro Blanco Peces and Jaime Merino

The Hubbard model on the kagome lattice is a widely used interacting model for describing the electronic properties of various transition-metal-based kagome materials. We find altermagnetism driven by Coulomb interaction in the kagome Hubbard model at Dirac filling with no spin-orbit coupling nor ex…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165143] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alejandro Blanco Peces and Jaime Merino

The Hubbard model on the kagome lattice is a widely used interacting model for describing the electronic properties of various transition-metal-based kagome materials. We find altermagnetism driven by Coulomb interaction in the kagome Hubbard model at Dirac filling with no spin-orbit coupling nor ex…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165143] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026

item.title: Itinerant magnetism in twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Liangtao Peng, Christophe De Beule, Yiyang Lai, Du Li, Li Yang, E. J. Mele, and Shaffique Adam

Using a self-consistent Hartree-Fock theory, we show that the recently observed ferromagnetism in twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ [Nat. Commun. 16, 1959 (2025)] can be understood as a Stoner-like instability of interaction-renormalized moiré bands. We quantitatively reproduce the observed Lifsh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161119] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Liangtao Peng, Christophe De Beule, Yiyang Lai, Du Li, Li Yang, E. J. Mele, and Shaffique Adam

Using a self-consistent Hartree-Fock theory, we show that the recently observed ferromagnetism in twisted bilayer WSe2 [Nat. Commun. 16, 1959 (2025)] can be understood as a Stoner-like instability of interaction-renormalized moiré bands. We quantitatively reproduce the observed Lifshitz transition a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161119] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Itinerant magnetism in twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ and ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$

item.title: Intrinsic second-order topological superconductors with tunable Majorana zero modes
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Jiao Wang, Yijie Mo, Zhi Wang, Zhigang Wu, and Zhongbo Yan

Dirac semimetals, with their protected Dirac points, present an ideal platform for realizing intrinsic topological superconductivity. In this work, we investigate superconductivity in a two-dimensional, square-lattice nonsymmorphic Dirac semimetal. In the normal state near half-filling, the Fermi su…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144513] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026

Dirac semimetals, with their protected Dirac points, present an ideal platform for realizing intrinsic topological superconductivity. In this work, we investigate superconductivity in a two-dimensional, square-lattice nonsymmorphic Dirac semimetal. In the normal state near half-filling, the Fermi su…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144513] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026

item.title: Deconfined quantum critical points in fermionic systems with spin-charge separation
item.description:
Author(s): Niccolò Baldelli, Arianna Montorsi, Sergi Julià-Farré, Maciej Lewenstein, Matteo Rizzi, and Luca Barbiero

Deconfined quantum critical points are intriguing transition points not predicted by the Landau-Ginzburg-Wilson symmetry-breaking paradigm which are usually identified by the appearance of a continuous phase transition between locally ordered phases. Here, we reveal the presence of deconfined quantu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165142] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Niccolò Baldelli, Arianna Montorsi, Sergi Julià-Farré, Maciej Lewenstein, Matteo Rizzi, and Luca Barbiero

Deconfined quantum critical points are intriguing transition points not predicted by the Landau-Ginzburg-Wilson symmetry-breaking paradigm which are usually identified by the appearance of a continuous phase transition between locally ordered phases. Here, we reveal the presence of deconfined quantu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165142] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026

item.title: Chaos, thermalization, and breakdown of quantum-classical correspondence in a collective many-body system
item.description:
Author(s): Ángel L. Corps, Sebastián Gómez, Pavel Stránský, Armando Relaño, and Pavel Cejnar

We investigate thermalization and the quantum-classical correspondence in the collective Bose-Hubbard model, focusing on the four-site case. Our analysis of the classical phase-space structure and its excited-state quantum phase transitions leads us to three dynamical regimes: symmetry-breaking low-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144315] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026

We investigate thermalization and the quantum-classical correspondence in the collective Bose-Hubbard model, focusing on the four-site case. Our analysis of the classical phase-space structure and its excited-state quantum phase transitions leads us to three dynamical regimes: symmetry-breaking low-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144315] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026

item.title: Floquet-engineered Chern insulator in two-dimensional ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-wave altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Hosein Cheraghchi

We investigate Floquet-engineered topological phases in two-dimensional ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-wave altermagnets irradiated by circularly polarized light in the off-resonant regime. These materials exhibit large momentum-dependent spin splitting governed by distinctive magnetic symmetries. Using a …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155439] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hosein Cheraghchi

We investigate Floquet-engineered topological phases in two-dimensional dx2−y2-wave altermagnets irradiated by circularly polarized light in the off-resonant regime. These materials exhibit large momentum-dependent spin splitting governed by distinctive magnetic symmetries. Using a lattice model com…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155439] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026

item.title: Antiferromagnetic Chern insulator phase in the Kane-Mele-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Bao-Qing Wang, Can Shao, Takami Tohyama, Hong-Gang Luo, and Hantao Lu

The emergence of the antiferromagnetic (AFM) Chern insulator (AFCI) phase in the Kane-Mele-Hubbard (KMH) model with a finite sublattice potential is investigated. The AFCI, characterized by AFM correlations coexisting with quantized Hall conductance, has long raised the question of whether it can ex…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165141] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bao-Qing Wang, Can Shao, Takami Tohyama, Hong-Gang Luo, and Hantao Lu

The emergence of the antiferromagnetic (AFM) Chern insulator (AFCI) phase in the Kane-Mele-Hubbard (KMH) model with a finite sublattice potential is investigated. The AFCI, characterized by AFM correlations coexisting with quantized Hall conductance, has long raised the question of whether it can ex…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165141] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Antiferromagnetic Chern insulator phase in the Kane-Mele-Hubbard model

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Electrical readout of topological spin order in ultrathin insulators with small magnetization
item.description:
Author(s): Jing Li, Huilin Lai, Andrew H. Comstock, Aeron McConnell, Bharat Giri, Yu Yun, Tianhao Zhao, Xiao Wang, Yongseong Choi, Xuemei Cheng, Jian Shen, Zhigang Jiang, Dali Sun, Wenbin Wang, and Xiaoshan Xu

Here, the authors demonstrate that the interfacial topological Hall effect enables an electrical readout of topological spin order in insulating magnets with extremely small magnetization. In Pt/hexagonal LuFeO3 heterostructures, the spin topology from noncoplanar canting is imprinted onto Pt via magnetic proximity, producing a large Hall response detectable down to the ultrathin limit of 1.5 unit cells. This approach provides a sensitive probe of otherwise inaccessible magnetic order in ultrathin insulating systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134436] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jing Li, Huilin Lai, Andrew H. Comstock, Aeron McConnell, Bharat Giri, Yu Yun, Tianhao Zhao, Xiao Wang, Yongseong Choi, Xuemei Cheng, Jian Shen, Zhigang Jiang, Dali Sun, Wenbin Wang, and Xiaoshan Xu

Here, the authors demonstrate that the interfacial topological Hall effect enables an electrical readout of topological spin order in insulating magnets with extremely small magnetization. In Pt/hexagonal LuFeO3 heterostructures, the spin topology from noncoplanar canting is imprinted onto Pt via magnetic proximity, producing a large Hall response detectable down to the ultrathin limit of 1.5 unit cells. This approach provides a sensitive probe of otherwise inaccessible magnetic order in ultrathin insulating systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134436] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrical readout of topological spin order in ultrathin insulators with small magnetization
item.dc:creator: Jing Li, Huilin Lai, Andrew H. Comstock, Aeron McConnell, Bharat Giri, Yu Yun, Tianhao Zhao, Xiao Wang, Yongseong Choi, Xuemei Cheng, Jian Shen, Zhigang Jiang, Dali Sun, Wenbin Wang, and Xiaoshan Xu

item.title: Quantum geometric contribution to the diffusion constant in metals and semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): A. A. Burkov

We discuss the quantum geometric contribution to the diffusion constant and the DC conductivity in metals and semimetals with linear Dirac dispersion. We demonstrate that, for systems with perfectly linear dispersion, there exists a clear and rigorous separation of the quantum geometric from the ord…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155145] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

We discuss the quantum geometric contribution to the diffusion constant and the DC conductivity in metals and semimetals with linear Dirac dispersion. We demonstrate that, for systems with perfectly linear dispersion, there exists a clear and rigorous separation of the quantum geometric from the ord…

item.title: Mobile impurity in the Kitaev chain: Phase diagram and signatures of topology
item.description:
Author(s): A. V. Sadovnikov, M. S. Bahovadinov, A. N. Rubtsov, and A. A. Markov

We study the physics of a mobile impurity immersed in a one-dimensional topological superconductor. We discuss the phase diagram of this system obtained with exact diagonalization and Chevy-type variational Ansatz. We argue that the character of the transition from a weak to strong coupling regime d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155146] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. V. Sadovnikov, M. S. Bahovadinov, A. N. Rubtsov, and A. A. Markov

We study the physics of a mobile impurity immersed in a one-dimensional topological superconductor. We discuss the phase diagram of this system obtained with exact diagonalization and Chevy-type variational Ansatz. We argue that the character of the transition from a weak to strong coupling regime d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155146] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Mobile impurity in the Kitaev chain: Phase diagram and signatures of topology
item.dc:creator: A. V. Sadovnikov, M. S. Bahovadinov, A. N. Rubtsov, and A. A. Markov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Tunable Josephson diode effect mediated by topological surface states in ${\mathrm{BiSbTeSe}}_{2}$ Josephson junctions
item.description:
Author(s): Si-Li Wu, Zhi-Hui Ren, Peng Zhu, Hao-Chen Zhang, Xue-Tao Di, Chong Wang, Chuan Li, Zhiwei Wang, Cai-Zhen Li, and Zhi-Min Liao

Here, the authors demonstrate a gate-tunable Josephson diode effect (JDE) mediated by topological surface states in high-transparency Nb/BiSbTeSe2/Nb junctions. Temperature-dependent measurements show that the JDE is primarily driven by surface states, with bulk states contributing to the supercurrent but not to the diode effect. Gate tuning further enhances the surface contribution, increasing the diode efficiency from 40% to 50%. These findings establish topological surface states as a promising platform for efficient tunable superconducting diodes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155435] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Si-Li Wu, Zhi-Hui Ren, Peng Zhu, Hao-Chen Zhang, Xue-Tao Di, Chong Wang, Chuan Li, Zhiwei Wang, Cai-Zhen Li, and Zhi-Min Liao

Here, the authors demonstrate a gate-tunable Josephson diode effect (JDE) mediated by topological surface states in high-transparency Nb/BiSbTeSe2/Nb junctions. Temperature-dependent measurements show that the JDE is primarily driven by surface states, with bulk states contributing to the supercurrent but not to the diode effect. Gate tuning further enhances the surface contribution, increasing the diode efficiency from 40% to 50%. These findings establish topological surface states as a promising platform for efficient tunable superconducting diodes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155435] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunable Josephson diode effect mediated by topological surface states in ${\mathrm{BiSbTeSe}}_{2}$ Josephson junctions
item.dc:creator: Si-Li Wu, Zhi-Hui Ren, Peng Zhu, Hao-Chen Zhang, Xue-Tao Di, Chong Wang, Chuan Li, Zhiwei Wang, Cai-Zhen Li, and Zhi-Min Liao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Proposal for resolving quantized Landau orbits via elastic XUV scattering
item.description:
Author(s): Sabrina Meyer, Joris Sturm, Christina Schröder, Stephen Hughes, Andreas Knorr, and Lara Greten

In a strong magnetic field, a 2D electron gas is quantized into discrete Landau levels, the quantum version of cyclotron motion. Based on a microscopic theory, the authors propose here scattering with extreme-ultraviolet light as an optical probe of Landau-orbit structure. They isolate spatial Landau-orbit information by normalizing to a zero-magnetic-field reference. This provides access to the probability density distributions of individual Landau-Level wave functions, featuring radial maxima at the quantized Larmor radii.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165421] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sabrina Meyer, Joris Sturm, Christina Schröder, Stephen Hughes, Andreas Knorr, and Lara Greten

In a strong magnetic field, a 2D electron gas is quantized into discrete Landau levels, the quantum version of cyclotron motion. Based on a microscopic theory, the authors propose here scattering with extreme-ultraviolet light as an optical probe of Landau-orbit structure. They isolate spatial Landau-orbit information by normalizing to a zero-magnetic-field reference. This provides access to the probability density distributions of individual Landau-Level wave functions, featuring radial maxima at the quantized Larmor radii.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165421] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Proposal for resolving quantized Landau orbits via elastic XUV scattering
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Meyer, Joris Sturm, Christina Schröder, Stephen Hughes, Andreas Knorr, and Lara Greten
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Fermion parity and quantum capacitance oscillation with partially separated Majorana and quasi-Majorana modes
item.description:
Author(s): Tudor D. Stanescu and Sumanta Tewari

Quantum capacitance measurements of a hybrid semiconductor-superconductor nanowire coupled to a quantum dot can enable the determination of the shared fermion parity of Majorana zero modes. Here, the authors demonstrate that, in general, the observation of the corresponding parity-dependent quantum capacitance oscillations, as observed in recent experiments, indicates the presence of partially separated Majorana modes or topologically trivial quasi-Majorana modes, and does not represent a unique signature of topologically protected Majorana zero modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165422] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tudor D. Stanescu and Sumanta Tewari

Quantum capacitance measurements of a hybrid semiconductor-superconductor nanowire coupled to a quantum dot can enable the determination of the shared fermion parity of Majorana zero modes. Here, the authors demonstrate that, in general, the observation of the corresponding parity-dependent quantum capacitance oscillations, as observed in recent experiments, indicates the presence of partially separated Majorana modes or topologically trivial quasi-Majorana modes, and does not represent a unique signature of topologically protected Majorana zero modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165422] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Fermion parity and quantum capacitance oscillation with partially separated Majorana and quasi-Majorana modes
item.dc:creator: Tudor D. Stanescu and Sumanta Tewari
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Symmetry-governed flat band fragmentation in a kagome moiré quasicrystal
item.description:
Author(s): Yongdao Chen, Laiyuan Su, Yifan Gao, Lede Xian, and Li Huang

Moiré quasicrystals extend the twistronics paradigm beyond crystalline order, hosting electronic properties dictated by nonperiodic symmetry. Here, we investigate the fate of the kagome lattice's defining feature, its flat band, in a moiré quasicrystal formed by a ${30}^{∘}$-twisted bilayer. Our tig…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165423] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongdao Chen, Laiyuan Su, Yifan Gao, Lede Xian, and Li Huang

Moiré quasicrystals extend the twistronics paradigm beyond crystalline order, hosting electronic properties dictated by nonperiodic symmetry. Here, we investigate the fate of the kagome lattice's defining feature, its flat band, in a moiré quasicrystal formed by a 30∘-twisted bilayer. Our tight-bind…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165423] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-governed flat band fragmentation in a kagome moiré quasicrystal

item.title: $1s2p$ resonant inelastic x-ray scattering natural circular dichroism
item.description:

Natural circular dichroism (NCD) provides fundamental insight into molecular chirality and stereochemical environments. Extending this concept into the hard-x-ray regime enables element-specific and site-selective chiral sensitivity inaccessible to optical wavelengths. Here, we demonstrate that $1s2…

Natural circular dichroism (NCD) provides fundamental insight into molecular chirality and stereochemical environments. Extending this concept into the hard-x-ray regime enables element-specific and site-selective chiral sensitivity inaccessible to optical wavelengths. Here, we demonstrate that 1s2p…

item.title: Three-dimensional spin susceptibility in ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{0.75}{\mathrm{K}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$: Out-of-plane modulation revealed by neutron spectroscopy and theoretical modeling
item.description:
Author(s): Naoki Murai, Katsuhiro Suzuki, Masamichi Nakajima, Maiko Kofu, Seiko Ohira-Kawamura, Yasuhiro Inamura, and Ryoichi Kajimoto

We present a combined experimental and theoretical investigation of the spin dynamics in the iron-based superconductor ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{0.75}{\mathrm{K}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$. Time-of-flight inelastic neutron scattering measurements reveal the three-dimensional (3D) nature of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134430] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Naoki Murai, Katsuhiro Suzuki, Masamichi Nakajima, Maiko Kofu, Seiko Ohira-Kawamura, Yasuhiro Inamura, and Ryoichi Kajimoto

We present a combined experimental and theoretical investigation of the spin dynamics in the iron-based superconductor Ba0.75K0.25Fe2As2. Time-of-flight inelastic neutron scattering measurements reveal the three-dimensional (3D) nature of the spin fluctuations, manifested as out-of-plane modulations…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134430] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Noncollinear ferromagnetism in the Kondo-lattice compound ${\mathrm{Ce}}_{5}{\mathrm{CoGe}}_{2}$

Author(s): Jinyu Wu, Jiawen Zhang, Toni Shiroka, Shams Sohel Islam, Mingyi Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Devashibhai T. Adroja, Yu Liu, Huiqiu Yuan, and Michael Smidman

The dense Kondo lattice ${\mathrm{Ce}}_{5}{\mathrm{CoGe}}_{2}$ exhibits superconductivity after the magnetic ordering is suppressed by pressure. Here the ambient pressure magnetic state is investigated via magnetization, heat capacity, powder neutron diffraction, and muon-spin relaxation measurement…

Author(s): Jinyu Wu, Jiawen Zhang, Toni Shiroka, Shams Sohel Islam, Mingyi Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Devashibhai T. Adroja, Yu Liu, Huiqiu Yuan, and Michael Smidman

The dense Kondo lattice Ce5CoGe2 exhibits superconductivity after the magnetic ordering is suppressed by pressure. Here the ambient pressure magnetic state is investigated via magnetization, heat capacity, powder neutron diffraction, and muon-spin relaxation measurements. Neutron diffraction results…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134431] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Rashba spin-orbit coupling and artificially engineered topological superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Sankar Das Sarma, Katharina Laubscher, Haining Pan, Jay D. Sau, and Tudor D. Stanescu

Rashba spin–orbit coupling (SOC), introduced in seminal works by Emmanuel Rashba, plays a central role in engineered topological superconductivity. In this Perspective, the authors review how Rashba SOC enables the realization of low-dimensional topological superconductors supporting Majorana zero modes, the key building blocks for topological quantum computation. They highlight how tuning the Rashba SOC can enhance the topological gap, and survey material platforms and design strategies where Rashba SOC is leveraged to create and control topological superconducting phases.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139604] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sankar Das Sarma, Katharina Laubscher, Haining Pan, Jay D. Sau, and Tudor D. Stanescu

Rashba spin–orbit coupling (SOC), introduced in seminal works by Emmanuel Rashba, plays a central role in engineered topological superconductivity. In this Perspective, the authors review how Rashba SOC enables the realization of low-dimensional topological superconductors supporting Majorana zero modes, the key building blocks for topological quantum computation. They highlight how tuning the Rashba SOC can enhance the topological gap, and survey material platforms and design strategies where Rashba SOC is leveraged to create and control topological superconducting phases.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139604] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Rashba spin-orbit coupling and artificially engineered topological superconductors
item.dc:creator: Sankar Das Sarma, Katharina Laubscher, Haining Pan, Jay D. Sau, and Tudor D. Stanescu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Technical Perspective
item.prism:section: Technical Perspective

item.title: Hidden magnon Berry curvature drives vertical magnon transport
item.description:
Author(s): Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

The authors show here that quasi-two-dimensional magnetic insulators comprising a few layers are fundamentally distinct, at the quantum geometric level, from perfectly flat single-layered 2D magnets. They discover that magnon excitations in such quasi-2D insulators experience in-plane components of a bosonic symplectic Berry curvature, absent in flat 2D magnetic insulators. They call this the “hidden magnon Berry curvature” (HMBC). They propose that HMBC can generate (and be detected via) magnon transport perpendicular to the layers of van der Waals materials in response to thermal or magnetic-field gradients. Their work points toward the possibility of yet-undiscovered hidden quantum geometric transport in layered quasi-2D magnets.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144426] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

The authors show here that quasi-two-dimensional magnetic insulators comprising a few layers are fundamentally distinct, at the quantum geometric level, from perfectly flat single-layered 2D magnets. They discover that magnon excitations in such quasi-2D insulators experience in-plane components of a bosonic symplectic Berry curvature, absent in flat 2D magnetic insulators. They call this the “hidden magnon Berry curvature” (HMBC). They propose that HMBC can generate (and be detected via) magnon transport perpendicular to the layers of van der Waals materials in response to thermal or magnetic-field gradients. Their work points toward the possibility of yet-undiscovered hidden quantum geometric transport in layered quasi-2D magnets.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144426] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Hidden magnon Berry curvature drives vertical magnon transport
item.dc:creator: Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

Recent experiments have reported chiral time-reversal broken superconductivity in $n$-layer rhombohedral graphene for $n=4,\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}5,\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\text{and}\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}6$. Introducing a moiré potential by alignment with a hexagonal boron nitride sub…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144510] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yunchao Zhang, Leyna Shackleton, and T. Senthil

Recent experiments have reported chiral time-reversal broken superconductivity in n-layer rhombohedral graphene for n=4,5,and6. Introducing a moiré potential by alignment with a hexagonal boron nitride substrate suppresses the superconductivity but leads instead to various fractional quantum anomalo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144510] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Pathways from a chiral superconductor to a composite Fermi liquid

We construct asymptotic quantum many-body scars (AQMBS) in one-dimensional $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hubbard chains ($N≥3$) by embedding the scar subspace into an auxiliary Hilbert subspace ${\mathcal{H}}_{P}$ and identifying a parent Hamiltonian within it, together with a corresponding extension of the rest…

[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155137] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Higher-dimensional generalization of the Kitaev spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Po-Jui Chen and Piers Coleman

We construct an exactly solvable model of a four-dimensional Kitaev spin liquid. The lattice structure is orthorhombic and each unit cell contains six sublattice degrees of freedom. We demonstrate that the Fermi surface of the model is made up of two-dimensional surfaces. Additionally, we evaluate t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155140] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

We demonstrate that the Fermi surface of the model is made up of two-dimensional surfaces. Additionally, we evaluate t…

item.title: Optical response of the Dirac semimetals ${\mathrm{GdSb}}_{0.56}{\mathrm{Te}}_{1.35}$ and ${\mathrm{GdSb}}_{0.45}{\mathrm{Te}}_{1.50}$: Influence of charge density wave distortion and magnetic order
item.description:

In this work we use infrared spectroscopy to probe the temperature-dependent electro…

In this work we use infrared spectroscopy to probe the temperature-dependent electronic excitations in GdSb0.56Te1…

By comparing the electronic transmission in nanoribbons with edges along the crystallographic ŷ and x̂ directions, our analysis…

item.description:

We demonstrate how spin-orbit coupling (SOC) controls band topology and induces higher-order topological corner states in a two-dimensional Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model. SOC lifts spin degeneracy and, when applied along both lattice directions, generates robust zero-energy corner states. By matc…

The electronic structure of materials is fundamentally governed by their crystal symmetry. While most research on two-dimensional materials has focused on hexagonal lattices, such as graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. This work explores a square-lattice system: …

We show that an altermagnet and an in-plane magnetic field applied near one surface gap the corresponding Dirac cone, yielding an altermagnet-ind…

item.title: Visible and terahertz nonlinear responses in the topological noble metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): George J. de Coster, Lucas Lafeta, Stefan Heiserer, Cormac Ó Coileáin, Zdenek Sofer, Achim Hartschuh, Georg S. Duesberg, and Paul Seifert

Nonlinear processes can offer pathways to next-generation sensors and frequency mixing devices to overcome modern imaging, detection, and communication challenges. In this article, we report on strong second- and third-order nonlinear optical responses in visible and terahertz light in single crysta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165419] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): George J. de Coster, Lucas Lafeta, Stefan Heiserer, Cormac Ó Coileáin, Zdenek Sofer, Achim Hartschuh, Georg S. Duesberg, and Paul Seifert

Nonlinear processes can offer pathways to next-generation sensors and frequency mixing devices to overcome modern imaging, detection, and communication challenges. In this article, we report on strong second- and third-order nonlinear optical responses in visible and terahertz light in single crysta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165419] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Visible and terahertz nonlinear responses in the topological noble metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: George J. de Coster, Lucas Lafeta, Stefan Heiserer, Cormac Ó Coileáin, Zdenek Sofer, Achim Hartschuh, Georg S. Duesberg, and Paul Seifert
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Majorana zero modes in semiconductor-superconductor hybrid structures: Defining topology in short and disordered nanowires through Majorana splitting
item.description:
Author(s): Haining Pan and Sankar Das Sarma

Majorana zero modes (MZMs) are bound midgap topological excitations at the ends of a one-dimensional (1D) topological superconductor, which must come in pairs. If the two MZMs in the pair are sufficiently well-separated by a distance much larger than their individual localization lengths, then the M…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165420] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haining Pan and Sankar Das Sarma

Majorana zero modes (MZMs) are bound midgap topological excitations at the ends of a one-dimensional (1D) topological superconductor, which must come in pairs. If the two MZMs in the pair are sufficiently well-separated by a distance much larger than their individual localization lengths, then the M…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165420] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Majorana zero modes in semiconductor-superconductor hybrid structures: Defining topology in short and disordered nanowires through Majorana splitting
item.dc:creator: Haining Pan and Sankar Das Sarma
